name: Cccs Of The Savannah Area, Inc.- Sc Branch
description_short: Non-profit credit counseling agency in Savannah offering debt management, credit counseling, and housing assistance. Locally-based with 60 years of service.
description_long:
CCCS of Savannah is a locally-based, non-profit credit counseling agency that has served the Savannah area for over 60 years. The organization emphasizes its status as the only locally-based non-profit credit counseling agency in the region and reports helping hundreds of thousands of people restore their financial health. The agency operates as a legitimate consumer finance resource focused on education and structured debt reduction rather than predatory lending or quick fixes.

The organization offers a comprehensive suite of services including debt management programs, credit and budget counseling, housing counseling with certified counselors, first-time homebuyer assistance, reverse mortgage guidance, and foreclosure prevention counseling. They provide a "My Virtual Counselor" tool allowing clients to estimate savings from debt management plans before committing to counseling. Initial consultations and online appointments are offered free of charge, with certified counselors available to discuss individual financial situations.

CCCS distinguishes itself through its non-profit structure, emphasis on certified counselors, no-judgment environment, and community-focused approach. The organization highlights its ability to negotiate lower interest rates and manageable payment terms with creditors as part of their debt management programs. Their website features multiple client testimonials spanning years of service, including clients who paid off significant debt ($20,000+ balances) through coordinated payment plans. The agency positions itself as a judgment-free alternative to navigating debt alone.

As a legitimate non-profit counseling service, CCCS of Savannah represents appropriate use of credit counseling for consumers struggling with unsecured debt and housing challenges. However, prospective clients should understand that debt management plans typically require 3-5 years of commitment and may involve creditor negotiations that affect credit reports. The organization is suitable for those seeking education and structured solutions rather than debt elimination or quick debt relief.
pros: Non-profit organization with 60-year track record in Savannah area, reducing conflicts of interest
Free initial consultations and online appointment scheduling available
Certified housing counselors available for foreclosure prevention and first-time homebuyer programs
Debt management plans include creditor rate negotiation to reduce interest and create manageable payments
Multiple long-term client success stories documented, including customers paying off $20,000+ balances
No-judgment environment explicitly emphasized, addressing common consumer hesitation about seeking help
Virtual counselor tool allows clients to estimate savings before formal counseling commitment
cons: Debt management programs require multi-year commitment (4+ years documented in testimonials), not fast debt elimination
Services appear limited to Savannah area geographically despite national non-profit network potential
Website does not clearly disclose specific creditor participation rates or typical interest rate reductions achieved
No transparent pricing structure or fee schedule visible on website for debt management services
Debt management plan enrollment may negatively impact credit scores during repayment period (not disclosed on site)
best_for: Consumers with $5,000-$50,000 in credit card debt seeking structured multi-year repayment with professional creditor negotiation
First-time homebuyers in Savannah area needing education and access to down payment assistance programs
Homeowners facing foreclosure who want to explore alternatives before losing property
Individuals with multiple debts seeking comprehensive budget counseling and financial health restoration
diagnosis: CCCS of Savannah is best for Savannah-area residents carrying unsecured debt who need professional negotiation with creditors and can commit to 3-5 year repayment plans. The main caveat is that debt management plans are structured solutions requiring long-term commitment and financial discipline—they are not quick debt elimination programs, and participation may affect credit scores during the repayment period.
services: Free credit and budget counseling with certified counselors
Debt management programs with creditor negotiation and rate reduction
First-time homebuyer education and program connection
Reverse mortgage guidance and equity leverage consultation
My Virtual Counselor tool for debt management plan savings estimation
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
